summary: Verified the 'Add to order' click bug fix on the static snack menu site (http://localhost:8900/index.html). Used REAL physical mouse clicks (page.mouse.click at coordinates, not JS .click()) to reproduce the original scenario where the .card::before glow overlay was intercepting pointer events. The CSS fix (.card::before{pointer-events:none} + .card>*{position:relative;z-index:2}) is in place. All 6 requested scenarios pass: (1) primary bug — real click adds item; (2) multiple clicks on same card increment qty; (3) Add works on Capri Sun (Drinks), M&Ms (Candy), Takis (Chips), Trident Tropical (Gum); (4) qty +/- and remove buttons work; (5) nav & cart totals sync; (6) submit disabled when empty, enabled with items, and hidden Formspree fields (Ordered Items, Quantities, Total) populated correctly. Did not actually POST to Formspree.

issues: 'Pick your snacks' <h2> in .section-head is partially hidden behind the fixed .nav bar when scrolled to top of #menu (visible in screenshot — text is clipped by the translucent nav). Consider adding scroll-margin-top or padding-top to compensate for the fixed nav height.

critical_code_review_comments: Element at button center point after fix correctly returns tag=BUTTON class='add' (previously would have been the .card::before overlay). Fix is correctly implemented at index.html lines 165-167.
Minor: '.section-head h2' can be occluded by the fixed nav on scroll — consider `scroll-margin-top` for #menu or additional top padding.
Static site has no data-testid attributes (acceptable for a small static shop, but adding them to .add, #submitBtn, .li-remove, .qty buttons would improve future testability).
Formspree submission uses AJAX with proper success/error status handling — good.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Static site served at http://localhost:8900/index.html via python http.server. Two files only: /app/index.html and /app/snacks.js. The primary bug (decorative .card::before overlay intercepting clicks) is fixed via pointer-events:none on ::before and z-index:2 on .card>*. To validate the fix, ALWAYS use real coordinate-based mouse clicks (page.mouse.click(x, y)) — a JS .click() would bypass the overlay-hit issue entirely and give a false positive. All Add buttons across all 4 categories verified working.
rca of the issue: Root cause: The .card::before pseudo-element was an absolutely-positioned decorative radial-gradient glow spanning `inset:0` on top of the card. It had default pointer-events:auto, so it covered the entire card including the '+ Add to order' button. Real mouse clicks hit the ::before overlay (which had no click handler) and never reached the button. JS .click() calls the DOM element directly and bypasses hit-testing, which is why the bug wasn't caught in unit-style testing. Fix: 1) `.card::before{pointer-events:none}` lets clicks pass through the overlay; 2) `.card>*{position:relative;z-index:2}` ensures real card content (including the button) sits above the pseudo-element in the stacking context. Verification: elementFromPoint(cx, cy) at the button center now returns the BUTTON element (confirmed in test output), and page.mouse.click successfully triggers the addToCart handler.
